spec_match.rationale: The demo mirrors the manifest-incubations processing path: type origin entries, HTTPS normalization, association-file validation by manifest id, resolved extension scopes, and extended-scope navigation matching.

interactive.rationale: Users can edit the app identity, primary scope, extension entries, association-file states, scoped paths, and test URL while the map, artifacts, log, and before/after verdicts update live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still delivers the feature-index promise of mapping regional, billing, support, partner, and attacker surfaces into an installed app scope.

edge_cases.rationale: The page exposes rejected branches for non-HTTPS origins, unsupported entry types, missing association files, wrong manifest ids, duplicate origins, and URLs outside the validated path scope.

references.rationale: The page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/5746537956114432 link along with the manifest-incubations spec and explainer.

design_system.rationale: The local CSS uses shared variables for color, keeps button background and color paired, and presents the interactive lab without raw color literals.

summary: The previous ChromeStatus reference issue is fixed, and the concept now demonstrates the core scope_extensions validation and navigation behavior with realistic rejected branches.
